events.change_summary: Intent check: automated PyPI publishing + VCS versioning

events.summary: Intent check pushed: automated PyPI publishing via tag-triggered GitHub Actions workflow, VCS-derived versioning via hatch-vcs, and OIDC trusted publishing. Manual commit/push and one-time PyPI setup are called out as out of scope.

events.change_summary: Full scope: VCS versioning via hatch-vcs + OIDC-based PyPI publish workflow

events.rationale: No UI changes (skip design). Changes touch 4 files across the build config and CI pipeline — well-contained, single subsystem (packaging), building on existing hatchling patterns (skip architecture). Standard backend-feature pipeline is the right fit.

alternatives_considered: Custom version script reading git describe output (fragile, reinvents the wheel)
Hardcoded version managed by a release script (status quo — error-prone, requires manual file edits)
events.decision: Use hatch-vcs for VCS-based versioning instead of setuptools-scm or a manual version script
events.rationale: The project already uses hatchling as its build backend. hatch-vcs is hatchling's first-party VCS plugin, so it requires no additional tooling or backend changes — just adding it to [build-system].requires and configuring [tool.hatch.version]. setuptools-scm would require switching build backends or adding an awkward cross-tool integration.

alternatives_considered: PyPI API token stored as GitHub Actions secret (requires token rotation, leak risk)
Manually running twine upload locally (status quo — no automation)
events.decision: Use PyPI OIDC trusted publishing instead of a stored API token for the publish workflow
events.rationale: Trusted publishing requires no secrets in GitHub repository settings — the OIDC token is ephemeral and scoped to the specific workflow run. Stored API tokens must be rotated, can be leaked, and are permanently valid until manually revoked. The one-time browser setup in the PyPI UI is a reasonable trade-off for permanently eliminating token management overhead.

events.summary: Full scope written as revision 2. Single-phase implementation: (1) switch pyproject.toml to hatch-vcs dynamic versioning, (2) update __init__.py to import from the generated _version.py with a fallback, (3) add _version.py to .gitignore, (4) create .github/workflows/publish.yml with OIDC trusted publishing triggered on v*.*.* tags. No architecture stage needed — all changes are well-contained. Workflow classified as backend-feature / standard.

events.rationale: Routine sync drift: HEAD fast-forwarded (stale HEAD is a strict ancestor of current) with a byte-identical dirty-status digest; swept forward automatically without waking an agent.

events.note: Starting Phase 1: Wire up VCS versioning and automated publish

events.description: Starting phase 1: Wire up VCS versioning and automated publish

events.details: 14 tests passing (unchanged from baseline). VCS-derived version verified: 0.1.dev50+gc737241b7.d20260630. _version.py generated by hatch-vcs and gitignored. No ruff errors in changed files.

alternatives_considered: Single job combining build and publish (simpler but id-token permission is broader than needed)
Three jobs with separate lint/test step (unnecessary here since CI workflow already handles testing)
events.decision: Implementation: publish.yml splits build and publish into two separate jobs with artifact handoff
events.rationale: Scoping `id-token: write` only to the publish job follows least-privilege practice and is the pattern recommended by pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish. The build job has no OIDC permissions, so a compromised build step cannot request an OIDC token.

events.change_summary: Phase 1 complete: Wire up VCS versioning and automated publish

events.summary: Switched pyproject.toml to hatch-vcs dynamic versioning, updated __init__.py with try/except _version import, added _version.py to .gitignore, and created .github/workflows/publish.yml with two-job OIDC trusted publish workflow triggered on v*.*.* tags.

items.description: In the worktree root, run `uv pip install -e .` and confirm it succeeds with no errors.
Run `python -c "import netpath; print(netpath.__version__)"` and confirm it prints a non-empty version string (e.g. `0.1.dev50+gc737241b7.d20260630`).
Run `grep -c 'version = "0.1.0"' pyproject.toml` and confirm the output is `0`.
Run `grep 'hatch-vcs' pyproject.toml` and confirm it appears inside the `[build-system] requires` list.
Run `git status --short` and confirm `src/netpath/_version.py` does NOT appear as an untracked or modified file.

Open `.github/workflows/publish.yml` and verify: trigger is `on: push: tags: - "v*.*.*"`, `fetch-depth: 0` is present, `id-token: write` is set, and `pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1` is used with no `password:` input.

Wire up VCS versioning and automated publish

workflow.rationale: No UI changes (skip design). Changes touch 4 files across the build config and CI pipeline — well-contained, single subsystem (packaging), building on existing hatchling patterns (skip architecture). Standard backend-feature pipeline is the right fit.

stageContent.implementation:
## Phase 1: Wire up VCS versioning and automated publish

### Files Changed

- **`pyproject.toml`** — Added `hatch-vcs` to `[build-system].requires`; replaced `version = "0.1.0"` with `dynamic = ["version"]`; added `[tool.hatch.version]` (`source = "vcs"`) and `[tool.hatch.build.hooks.vcs]` (`version-file = "src/netpath/_version.py"`) sections.
- **`src/netpath/__init__.py`** — Replaced hardcoded `__version__ = "0.1.0"` with a try/except import from the generated `_version.py`, falling back to `"0.0.0+unknown"` when the file is absent.
- **`.gitignore`** — Appended `src/netpath/_version.py` so the generated file is never committed.
- **`.github/workflows/publish.yml`** — Created new two-job workflow: `build` checks out with `fetch-depth: 0`, builds sdist + wheel via `python -m build`, uploads dist/ as an artifact; `publish` downloads the artifact and runs `pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1` with `permissions: id-token: write`. Triggered on `v*.*.*` tag pushes.

### Verification Results

- `grep -c 'version = "0.1.0"' pyproject.toml` → 0 (no hardcoded version)
- `grep 'hatch-vcs' pyproject.toml` → matches in `[build-system].requires`
- `grep '_version.py' .gitignore` → matches
- `grep 'id-token: write' publish.yml` → matches
- `grep 'fetch-depth: 0' publish.yml` → matches
- Editable install generates `src/netpath/_version.py`; `git status --short` does not show it as untracked
- `python -c "import netpath; print(netpath.__version__)"` → `0.1.dev50+gc737241b7.d20260630`
- All 14 tests pass (unchanged from baseline)

### Deviations from Plan

None. Implementation follows the phase spec exactly.
